Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of The Order Denying Defendant?S Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Of California Casualty
Off Calendar Feb-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (by Zoom):
Nina Reynolds, Esq. 415-399-2900 Peterson Weyand & Martin LLP for Plaintiff.
Robyn Shields, Esq. 650-989-5412 Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman & Scheley for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. Ms. Shields contests and states they did not know the claim number, everything is privileged, there is case law subpoena should be detailed.
Ms Reynolds responds; liability claim, agrees with the court's analysis, claim numbers irrelevant, subpoena broad enough, don't have reports and evidence, damage issue
Court comments; need to know what to quash, confusion, motions stacked, "New Facts". The Court gives case managment advisement; extensive meet and confer, privilege log, appointment of a discovery referee, parties to agree as much as possible and narrow disputes.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Adra Young's Motion For Reconsideration Of The Order Denying Defendant's Motion To Quash Plaintiffs' Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Of California Casualty is DENIED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
